label: 1952 Bowman Large Football
description: The largest football card ever issued by Bowman in its original run, oversized at roughly 2-1/2 by 3-3/4 inches, with hand-colorized photographs that look more like miniature paintings than photos. Defined by its painterly portrait look, white border, and golden-age comic-book color palette. Sometimes called the most beautiful football set of the 1950s.

front.layout: Vertical card with a hand-tinted color photograph filling almost the entire front. Player is depicted in a posed action shot or three-quarter portrait, often without helmet. Below the image sits a thin colored facsimile signature box with the player's printed signature in script. No team logo on the front, no brand mark, no position label.
front.colors: Heavy use of saturated hand-painted hues: grass green (#4B8A3E), brick red (#A1382C), butter yellow (#E5C354), cobalt sky (#3A6FA8), and skin tones that read slightly waxy. White border roughly 1/8 inch wide.
front.typography: Player's printed signature in cursive script, dark blue or black on light cream signature panel. No other type on front.
front.photo_treatment: Hand-colorized black-and-white photo. Posed studio shots dominate: helmetless three-quarter turn, ball cradled, looking off-camera. Backgrounds are painted-in stadiums, blue skies, or solid color washes. Slight soft-focus painterly quality.
front.borders: Thin white border on all four sides, roughly 1/8 inch. No internal frame.
front.special_effects: None. Matte cardstock, no gloss.
front.distinctive_features: The painted-photograph look is unmistakable. Faces have a slightly waxy, idealized quality. Almost no on-card text other than the cursive signature.

back.layout: Vertical back. Card number in upper left in large bold numerals. Player name and position printed in block caps directly below. A short biographical paragraph occupies the upper third. Below that, a small career stats line or year-by-year mini-table. A trivia question or 'Did You Know' fact often runs at the bottom.
back.colors: Grayish off-white card stock with all text in single-color ink (black or dark brown). One accent color (usually red) for the card number and name banner.
back.typography: All sans-serif gothic block lettering for headers, classic serif body text for the bio. Stat headers in tiny condensed caps.
back.stat_block_format: One or two rows: previous year and career totals. Columns vary by position (Att, Yds, TD for runners; Comp, Att, Yds, TD for QBs).
back.bio_text: Third-person, almanac tone, 3-4 sentences. Mentions college, hometown, weight, height, prior season highlight.
back.card_number_treatment: Large card number top-left, formatted as 'NO. 47' inside a colored box or circle.
back.distinctive_features: Vintage almanac feel, single ink color, tiny trivia question at bottom.

front_prompt_template:
PHOTO INSTRUCTION (this overrides any photo direction below):
I am attaching a screenshot of the player — that uploaded image IS the card's photo.
Use it AS-IS. Do not generate a new player, do not change the pose, expression, face, body, hair, helmet, eyewear, jersey, number, or uniform, and do not replace it with a stock or AI-rendered player.
Treat the uploaded image as a locked photographic asset placed inside the card.
Apply ONLY the card design described below — frame, borders, color treatment / tint / grain, typography, name plate, team panel, badges, logos, finish (matte / gloss / foil / chrome / refractor), halftone, print artifacts, etc.
Crop and position the uploaded photo to fit the card layout, but do not redraw the player.

YEAR INSTRUCTION (this overrides any year reference in the design block below):
The year that appears PRINTED on the card itself — copyright line, set year, season label, "© {{year}} <brand>", year inside any badge/banner, anywhere a year shows up on the printed card face — must be {{year}}. The design references below may name an older era (e.g. "1952 Bowman", "1972 Topps") to describe the visual style; that is the AESTHETIC source only. Do not stamp the era year on the card. Any year visible to a viewer of the finished card must read {{year}}.

CARD DESIGN:
A vintage 1952 Bowman Large football trading card front, oversized 2.5 x 3.75 inch portrait orientation. The uploaded player photograph of {{name}}, rendered with a hand-colorized painted-photograph treatment, a {{position}} for {{school}}. The photo has the slightly waxy, idealized quality of mid-century hand-tinted photographs. Saturated period color palette: grass green, brick red, butter yellow, cream skin tones. Thin white border roughly 1/8 inch on all sides. At the bottom of the image, a cream-colored cursive facsimile signature reading '{{name}}' in dark blue script, no signature box outline. No team logo on front, no position label, no brand mark, no card number on front. Matte finish, no gloss, slight printed-paper texture. Subtle dot-pattern halftone visible on close inspection.

BACK DESIGN — render the back the way the real-world set actually looked
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The text under "BACK DESIGN" further down describes the era, brand, color palette, layout, typography, and finish. Match it.

CRITICAL ANTI-PATTERNS — these are the AI mistakes that make the back look fake; do NOT do any of these:

✗ DO NOT add stock clipart / cartoon icons next to stat rows (calendar icon for week, helmet icon for game, whistle icon for result, football icon for passing, cleat icon for rushing, etc.). REAL production sports cards do not put cartoon icons in front of stat rows. Ever.

✗ DO NOT lay out the back as a vertical "LABEL : value" dashboard, e.g. "WEEK : Week 8 / GAME : vs UTEP / FINAL : 63-3 / RESULT : Win / PASSING : 33/37 / RUSHING : 4 car". That is a phone-app screen, not a trading card. On real cards, the matchup goes in a small game-info ribbon and the player's numbers go in a tight tabular STAT PANEL with column headers across the top.

✗ DO NOT render the BACK CONTENT block above verbatim. It is REFERENCE DATA. Translate it into the visual language of the era:
    – Multi-year career data → render as a multi-row STAT TABLE with column headers (Year, GP, and position-specific stat columns) and one row per year, the way the brand's actual cards did. Bold/accent the highlight row.
    – Single-game data → render as a small TIGHT STAT PANEL: a tabular block (column headers + one row of numbers) or a single inline ribbon ("33/37 · 431 YDS · 7 TD"). Not a vertical labelled list.
    – Bio → render as a flowing 1-3 sentence paragraph in the era's typical tone, not as labeled fields.

✗ DO NOT generate filler AI-recap prose ("carved up", "controlled the game throughout", "dominant performance", "took the field with conviction"). Either keep recap copy short and factual, or omit it.

✓ DO match the brand's actual era — typography, color palette, panel shapes, photo treatment (or absence), card-stock color, finish. A 2017 Donruss Optic back looks different from a 1972 Topps back; respect that difference.

✓ DO use the data in the BACK CONTENT block as the source of truth for every number, name, and date on the back. Do not invent stats or claims not listed there.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACK DESIGN (the original set's visual language — populate it with the data above):
Reverse of a 1952 Bowman Large football card, vertical orientation. Off-white grayish cardstock background. Top-left corner: a red box containing the card number 'NO. {{number}}' in bold gothic block lettering. Directly to the right: player name '{{name}}' in solid black block caps, with '{{position}}' in smaller red caps below. Upper third dominated by a 3-4 sentence biographical paragraph in classic serif typography, third-person almanac tone, mentioning college ({{school}}), hometown, height, weight, and prior season highlights: {{bioText}}. Middle: a thin one-row stats line with tiny condensed caps headers and the player's career numbers: {{careerStatsTable}}. Below the stats: a brief 'DID YOU KNOW' trivia question in italic. Bottom edge: small copyright line in micro type. Single-color black ink throughout with red accents only on the card number and headers. No gloss, slight aged paper tone.
ai_generation_notes: Emphasize the hand-tinted painted quality of the photograph; AI will default to crisp modern color photo unless told to render it as 1950s colorized portrait painting. Reference 'hand-tinted black and white photograph' and 'painted backgrounds' explicitly. Avoid asking for action shots; this set is overwhelmingly posed.
iconic_examples: Frank Gifford rookie, Norm Van Brocklin, Hugh McElhenny rookie, Otto Graham.

label: 1956 Topps Football
description: Topps' first NFL set after acquiring Bowman, oversized at 2-5/8 by 3-5/8 inches. Defined by its bold solid-color team-coded backgrounds - every player on a team shares the same vivid background hue. Visually it sits between a comic book and a cigarette card.

front.layout: Vertical orientation. Player photo cut out and superimposed on a solid color background that corresponds to his team. Team logo (NFL team mark) sits in one upper corner. A horizontal name plate runs across the lower portion of the image: player name, team city, and position grouped together inside a thin white-bordered banner.
front.colors: Team-coded solid backgrounds: yellow for Bears, red for Lions, blue for Browns, green for Steelers, pink/salmon for Cardinals, etc. Bold saturated mid-century printing. Thin white aura traces the player's silhouette to separate him from the background.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif italic block caps inside the bottom name plate. Team city in slightly smaller upright caps. Position abbreviation in small caps below the name. All inside a white banner with thin black hairline border.
front.photo_treatment: Color photo of the player, cut out at the silhouette and dropped onto the solid color background. Mostly posed shots: three-quarter turn, helmet on or off, looking forward or off camera. Limited action photography; many shots are stiff studio poses against neutral backdrops then color-coded.
front.borders: Thin white border around the whole card, roughly 1/16 inch. Inside the border, the solid team color fills the rest of the front behind the cutout player.
front.special_effects: None. Matte cardstock with characteristic mid-50s ink-bubble printing imperfections.
front.distinctive_features: The team-coded solid color background. Knockout player silhouette with thin white aura. The cartoonish, comic-book vivid color palette.

back.layout: Vertical or horizontal back depending on player. Top portion has player name in large block caps with card number in a small box. Bio text fills the left half. A trivia cartoon (line drawing of a player or play with a question) takes up the right portion.
back.colors: Gray cardstock with red and black ink only. Cartoon is line-art black with red highlights.
back.typography: Block caps for headers, classic serif body text, small italic caps for stat headers.
back.stat_block_format: One or two rows of stats at the bottom: previous season and career totals. Position-specific columns.
back.bio_text: Almanac-style third person, 4-5 sentences. Mentions hometown, college, weight, height, prior team experience.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in upper-right corner in a small black or red circle, 'No. 47' format.
back.distinctive_features: The trivia cartoon with red-and-black line art. Two-color printing throughout.

Reverse of a 1956 Topps football card. Gray-cream cardstock. Two-color printing only: black and red ink. label: 1965 Topps Football (Tall Boys)
description: The legendary 'Tall Boys' set: oversized 2-1/2 by 4-11/16 inch cards featuring AFL players only (Topps had lost the NFL license). Bright vivid solid backgrounds, exaggerated vertical proportions, and one of the most iconic rookie cards of all time, the Joe Namath rookie. Visually unforgettable because of its tall awkward dimensions and saturated color blocks.

front.layout: Tall vertical orientation, much taller than wide (roughly 1:1.85). Large color player photo dominates the upper two-thirds. Solid color background fills the area behind the player. Team name in a horizontal stripe near the top. Player name and position in a small horizontal panel near the bottom.
front.colors: Team-coded solid backgrounds in vivid blocks: red, green, yellow, royal blue, or pink. Thin white border roughly 1/8 inch on all sides. The color-coded saturation reads almost like silkscreen poster art.
front.typography: Team name across the top in large bold block caps, white-on-color or black-on-color depending on background. Player name in bold sans-serif white block caps inside a colored bar near the bottom. Position in smaller all-caps below.
front.photo_treatment: Color action or posed shot of the player, generally helmet on or held under the arm. Many players shown mid-stride or with arm cocked. Knockout silhouette against the solid color background, similar to 1956 but taller.
front.borders: Thin white outer border on all four sides. No internal frame.
front.special_effects: None. Matte. Heavy ink-printed feel.
front.distinctive_features: The unmistakable extra-tall vertical format. Brightly saturated solid color backgrounds. Team name as a top stripe in large block caps.

back.layout: Vertical, divided into two main sections. Top: player name, position, vital stats (height, weight, age, college, hometown). Middle: a multi-line biography paragraph. Bottom: a vibrant cartoon illustration with a trivia question.
back.colors: Off-white cardstock with red and black ink. The cartoon is colorful with multiple flat hues, which is unusual for the era.
back.typography: Block sans-serif for headers, serif for biographical prose, condensed caps for stat headers.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year stats for skill players (multi-row table). For linemen, a one-line career summary or just bio without stats.
back.bio_text: Third-person almanac style, 5-7 sentences. Detailed for the era.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in upper corner, '47' or 'No. 47' format inside a small box.
back.distinctive_features: The vibrant cartoon at the bottom is a major identifier - full color, often comical.

label: 1972 Topps Football
description: The classic early-70s Topps look: a candy-stripe colored frame around an in-game color photo, with team name and player info inside a colored badge at the bottom. The set is also famous for its 'high number' series 3 short prints. The aesthetic is unapologetically 1972: chunky, blocky, and bright.

front.layout: Vertical card with a color photo occupying the upper roughly two-thirds, framed by a thick stylized colored border. Below the photo is a horizontal info panel containing the player name, team, and position, often in a stadium-pennant shape.
front.colors: Multi-colored frame typically combining two team-related colors in a candy-stripe or block pattern. Common combos: red/yellow, blue/orange, green/white. White outer border roughly 1/16 inch.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif block caps, often white drop-shadowed on a colored bar. Team name in slightly smaller caps. Position in small caps. All inside a horizontal pennant or rectangle below the photo.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo, frequently shot from sideline or end zone. Helmets on, mid-play. Some posed shots in vintage pre-game uniforms. Era-appropriate film grain.
front.borders: Thick stylized two-color frame around the photo, roughly 1/4 inch thick, often with a candy-stripe or block-pattern look. Thin white outer edge.
front.special_effects: None. Matte cardstock with characteristic 70s offset-print feel.
front.distinctive_features: The candy-stripe two-color frame is the dead giveaway. The pennant-shaped info panel at bottom.

back.layout: Horizontal back with player name and bio info on one side, year-by-year stat table dominating the other side. A small black-and-white cartoon trivia square sits in a corner.
back.colors: Pink-red and black ink on gray-green cardstock - a 70s Topps signature look.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stat columns, classic serif for the bio paragraph.
back.stat_block_format: Multi-row year-by-year stats with columns appropriate to position. Career totals row at bottom in bold.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in upper-left or upper-right inside a small black box, format '47'.
back.distinctive_features: The pink-and-green cardstock is a visual signature. Black-line cartoon with trivia question is small but always present.

label: 1981 Topps Football
description: The set with the iconic Joe Montana rookie. A 528-card base set defined by its pennant-flag bottom banner that holds player and team name. Topps did not have NFL logo rights, so helmet logos and team marks are airbrushed out - a hallmark of early-80s Topps football.

front.layout: Vertical card. Color photo fills nearly the full front. A pennant-shaped banner attached to a vertical 'flagpole' on the lower-left contains the team name. The pennant 'unfurls' to the right and includes the player's name in a second flag fold. Position label is in the white space below the banner. Topps logo appears in a corner - the first time Topps put its logo on a football card front.
front.colors: Multi-colored borders varying by team (each team uses its own colors). Pennant typically in a vivid solid hue with white reverse-out type. Background photo color is whatever the in-stadium shot looks like. White border at the very edge.
front.typography: Team name in bold sans-serif caps inside the pennant flag. Player name follows in the second fold of the pennant. Position abbreviation in small caps in white space below. Topps logo in italic script.
front.photo_treatment: Color in-game action or posed shot. Helmets visible but with logos airbrushed off. All-Pros from the prior season get a thin black stripe across the bottom of the photo.
front.borders: Thin white border on all four sides. The pennant graphic is the only internal frame element.
front.special_effects: None. Matte glossy cardstock for the era.
front.distinctive_features: The unfurled pennant graphic at bottom-left is unmistakable. The airbrushed-off helmet logos are an era signature.

back.layout: Horizontal back. The pennant motif is continued at the top - player name and card number sit inside pennant fold shapes. A list of vital stats (height, weight, college, hometown) follows. Year-by-year stats fill the middle. A small black-line cartoon trivia square sits to one side.
back.colors: Black and red ink on gray cardstock. A 1981 Topps signature.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stat columns, serif for the bio paragraph.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year multi-row stats for skill players. Season columns appropriate to position. Career row in bold at bottom.
back.bio_text: Third-person, 2-3 sentences integrated with vital stats.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number inside one of the pennant folds at top, format '#216'.
back.distinctive_features: The pennant motif carries from front to back. Two-color back printing (black and red on gray).

label: 1984 Topps Football
description: The John Elway rookie set. A 396-card base release with a clean white-bordered design and a colored color-block lower panel containing the player info. Marks Topps' return to a more conservative, photo-forward design after the more graphic 1981 and 1982 sets.

front.layout: Vertical. White outer border. Photo dominates the upper roughly 75 percent. A solid-color horizontal info bar runs across the bottom containing team name, then below that a separate strip with the player name in bold caps. Position label in a smaller box at the right.
front.colors: White outer border. Lower color bar in a team-coded color (Broncos orange, Cowboys blue, Steelers black, etc). Player name strip is white with black or color type.
front.typography: Team name in bold sans-serif block caps reverse-out white on the colored bar. Player name in bold black sans-serif caps on the white strip below. Position abbreviation in a small color-block at the right.
front.photo_treatment: Color action or posed photos, mid-action mostly. Helmet logos still airbrushed off (Topps still didn't have full team logo rights this year).
front.borders: Thin white border on all four sides, roughly 1/8 inch. The lower info bar acts as an internal frame element.
front.special_effects: None. Matte semi-gloss.
front.distinctive_features: The two-strip lower panel - colored bar above, white name bar below - is the visual signature. The clean white outer border.

back.layout: Vertical back. Top: card number, name, and biographical block. Middle: stat table with year-by-year career stats. Bottom: trivia cartoon and 'How to Pronounce' phonetic spelling for hard names.
back.colors: Green and black ink on white cardstock - a 1984 Topps signature.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stats, serif for bio.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year stat table, position-appropriate columns. Season totals row in bold.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in upper-left inside a small green box, format '63'.
back.distinctive_features: Green-and-black ink on white. The 'How to Pronounce' phonetic spelling for hard last names is a quirky era feature.

label: 1986 Topps Football
description: The green-bordered set with the Jerry Rice rookie. Famous for its solid emerald green border that wraps the entire card front, plus the first NFL cards of Steve Young and Reggie White. The green is so saturated it's the visual identity of the set on sight.

front.layout: Vertical. Thick solid green border around all four sides. Color photo inside. Below the photo, inside the green frame, a small white panel contains the player name and position. Team name appears at the top of the photo in a colored stripe.
front.colors: Saturated emerald-forest green border (#2D6A3F approximately). The dominant color overwhelms everything else.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif black caps inside a white panel at the bottom. Team name in white reverse-out caps in a colored stripe at the top of the photo. Position in small black caps below the name.
front.photo_treatment: Color action or posed shots. Mid-1980s helmet-on play photography. Helmet logos visible (Topps had NFL marks back by this point).
front.borders: Thick solid green border, roughly 3/16 inch wide on all sides. No internal frame beyond the green.
front.special_effects: None. Matte. Condition-sensitive - green borders show every chip.
front.distinctive_features: The all-encompassing emerald green border is unmistakable. Visually the most identifiable Topps set of the 80s.

back.layout: Vertical back. Card number top-left. Player name and bio at the top. Multi-row stats fill the middle. Black-line cartoon trivia at the bottom.
back.colors: Green and black ink on cream cardstock - green is carried through from the front.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stats, serif for bio.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in a green-outlined box upper-left, '#161' format.
back.distinctive_features: The green ink ties back to the front. Cream cardstock differs from the gray of earlier sets.

label: 1988 Topps Football
description: The Bo Jackson rookie set, defined by a thin double-bordered photo and a colored team-name strip across the bottom. A textbook late-80s Topps look: photo-forward, white outer border, decorative inner frame in team colors, with a small team helmet icon on the front for the first time in years.

front.layout: Vertical. White outer border. A thin colored inner frame (team-colored) outlines the photo. A horizontal team-color strip across the bottom holds the team name in large block caps. Player name and position appear in a smaller white panel below the strip. A small team helmet icon sits in the lower-right.
front.colors: White outer border. Inner frame color matched to team. Bottom team strip in team primary color with white reverse-out type. Helmet icon in full team colors.
front.typography: Team name in oversized bold sans-serif white caps in the bottom strip. Player name in bold black caps below. Position abbreviation in red caps.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photos with helmet logos visible (Topps had NFL marks). Mostly mid-play, sideline-shot photography.
front.borders: Thin white outer border roughly 1/16 inch. Thin colored inner frame around the photo (about 1/16 inch).
front.special_effects: None. Semi-glossy.
front.distinctive_features: Small team helmet icon in lower-right corner. Two-tier bottom panel with team name strip plus player info row.

back.layout: Horizontal. Player name and card number at top. Vital stats line. Year-by-year stat table on the right. Bio prose on the left. Cartoon trivia square at bottom.
back.colors: Black and red ink on cream cardstock.

label: 1989 Topps Football
description: A 396-card base set from Topps competing with the new Score and Pro Set entrants. Notable for its dotted/screen-pattern colored border that frames the photo, and its bold capital-letter name plate. Less iconic than its competitors that year but still a clean recognizable design.

front.layout: Vertical. Color photo dominates the upper portion, surrounded by a screen-dot patterned colored border. Below the photo, a thick solid color bar holds the team name, with the player's name and position in a small white strip at the very bottom.
front.colors: Border in a team-coded screened color (a fine dot pattern in the team's primary hue). Bottom bar in solid team color with reverse-out white type.
front.typography: Team name in bold sans-serif white block caps in the solid color bar. Player name in bold black caps in the bottom white strip. Position in red caps.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photos, helmet on, mid-play. Late-80s sideline photography with team logos visible.
front.borders: Thick screen-dot patterned colored border around the photo, roughly 3/16 inch.
front.special_effects: None.
front.distinctive_features: The screen-dot patterned border is the visual signature.

back.layout: Horizontal. Card number top-left. Bio and stats split across the back.
back.colors: Black and red ink on cream cardstock.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stats, serif for bio.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year stats with bold career totals.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in upper-left in a small red box, '#7' format.

label: 1989 Score Football
description: Score's debut football set and a hobby landmark. 330 cards with a clean modern design featuring team-colored borders and a small team helmet icon on the front, plus on-card rookie photography for one of the greatest rookie classes ever (Sanders, Aikman, Sanders, Thomas, Irvin). Borders chip easily - a notorious condition issue.

front.layout: Vertical. A thick team-colored border wraps the entire card front. Inside the border, the photo fills the top roughly 80 percent. A smaller white panel at the bottom holds the player name and position, with a small team helmet icon to the side.
front.colors: Team-coded thick borders: green for Jets, navy for Cowboys, red for 49ers, etc. Inside: the photo plus a clean white name panel.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif black caps inside the white panel. Position in smaller red caps. Score brand mark and 'NFL' wordmark in a corner of the white panel.
front.photo_treatment: On-card rookie photography is the signature - proper crisp posed/action shots of rookies in their rookie-year uniforms. Color action photos for veterans.
front.borders: Thick team-colored solid border roughly 1/4 inch wide on all four sides. No outer white border.
front.special_effects: None. Glossy front, thin cardstock that chips at corners.
front.distinctive_features: Thick saturated team-colored border. Small team helmet icon in the bottom panel.

back.layout: Vertical back. Top: a secondary photo of the player (smaller portrait or different action shot). Below: card number, player name, vital stats. Middle: a multi-paragraph career summary. Bottom: stat table.
back.colors: Full color back - unusual for the era. White cardstock with black body type, color photo at top, and team-colored accent bars.
back.typography: Sans-serif throughout. Bold caps for headers, regular weight for prose, condensed caps for stat columns.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year career stats with bold career totals.
back.bio_text: Third-person, 5-6 sentences. More prose-focused than the era's Topps backs.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in a colored box upper-left, '#7' format.
back.distinctive_features: The second photo on the back was a hobby novelty in 1989. The full-color back stands out from competitors who used 2-color printing.

label: 1989 Pro Set Football
description: Pro Set's debut, the first 'official NFL card.' 561 cards across three series. The series-1 cards have multi-colored team-toned perimeter borders; series-2 cards swap to a distinctive red border. The set is known for its bright photography and oversized in-action photo treatment.

front.layout: Vertical. A perimeter border that combines a team-helmet rectangle, player info, and NFL logo runs across the top, with the action photo dominating the rest of the card. The bottom of the photo is anchored by 'Pro Set' wordmark and 'The Official NFL Card' tagline.
front.colors: Series-1 borders are team-color-matched (varies by team). Series-2 borders all switch to a solid red. Photo is full-color in-action.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white caps inside a colored top bar with the team helmet at left. Team nickname appears beside the name. Position in small caps. 'PRO SET' wordmark across the bottom in italic block caps with 'The Official NFL Card' subhead.
front.photo_treatment: Bright, well-lit color action photography with helmet on. The set is known for crisp action shots - many considered some of the best 1989 photo content.
front.borders: Team-colored top bar roughly 1/4 inch tall containing team helmet and player name. Bottom bar with brand wordmark. Side borders thin in matching color. Series-2 cards swap all border to red.
front.special_effects: None. Glossy front.
front.distinctive_features: The team helmet integrated into the top header bar with the player name. The 'Pro Set' italic wordmark with 'The Official NFL Card' tagline.

back.layout: Horizontal back. Small player photo upper-left, vital stats and bio to the right, year-by-year stat table at the bottom.
back.colors: Black and team-colored ink on white cardstock.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stats, serif for bio prose.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year career stats, columns position-appropriate, with career totals.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number top-left in a colored box, '#42' format.
back.distinctive_features: Small player photo on the back is a Pro Set signature. Full-color printing on backs.

label: 1990 Pro Set Football
description: The mass-produced sequel to 1989's debut, with a refined design and a series of memorable inserts including Pro Bowl 106-card subset and Super Bowl MVP series. Visually a sleeker take on the 1989 layout - the team-color top bar gets thinner, the bottom 'Pro Set' wordmark plate gets larger, and the photos are bigger and more dynamic.

front.layout: Vertical. Player photo fills most of the front. A thin colored top bar contains the team helmet, player name, and position. A larger bottom plate contains the 'Pro Set' brand panel with team city/nickname.
front.colors: Team-color top bar matched to the player's team. White outer border thin. Bottom plate in white with team-color highlights.
front.typography: Player name in white block caps inside the top bar, with team helmet to the left. 'PRO SET' wordmark in italic block on the bottom plate.
front.photo_treatment: Glossy color action photography, generally larger and more dynamic than 1989. Helmet logos visible.
front.borders: Thin white outer edge. Top and bottom info bars frame the photo.
front.special_effects: None on base. The Pro Bowl insert subset has special graphic treatment.

back.distinctive_features: Carries the secondary-photo signature of the 1989 set.

label: 1991 Topps Stadium Club Football
description: The football set that elevated trading card photography. Topps printed on Kodak photo paper with a glossy premium finish. The signature look: full-bleed photo with no border at all, plus a tiny gold-foil signature ribbon at the bottom containing the player name and Stadium Club logo. The Brett Favre rookie (with name misspelled 'Farve') is the headline card.

front.layout: Vertical. Full-bleed color photograph fills the entire card, edge to edge, with NO outer border. Near the bottom, a small thin horizontal ribbon containing player name and Stadium Club logo, accented in gold foil.
front.colors: Whatever color the photograph contains. The signature ribbon is dark with gold foil highlights.
front.typography: Player name in white sans-serif caps inside the ribbon, Stadium Club logo in script. The 'STADIUM CLUB' wordmark in gold foil block caps.
front.photo_treatment: Premium Kodak-printed action and posed photography. Bright crisp colors, sharp detail, often shot mid-play with motion frozen. Backgrounds frequently include in-stadium crowd shots, sideline color, etc.
front.borders: NO border. Full bleed. The card is essentially a printed photograph.
front.special_effects: Gold-foil accents on the bottom ribbon. Glossy Kodak photo finish.
front.distinctive_features: The full-bleed no-border photo is the headline feature. The tiny gold-foil ribbon at bottom with Stadium Club logo is the only graphic element.

back.layout: Vertical. The back features a 'rookie card' image of the player at the top - a small reproduction of the player's actual rookie card from years past. Below that, biographical text and a single-line career stat summary on a colored field-pattern backdrop.
back.colors: The back has a colorful field/grass-pattern backdrop with green and white accents. White text where needed.
back.typography: Bold sans-serif throughout. Caps for headers, regular weight for body.
back.stat_block_format: Career-only single-line stat summary; not the year-by-year table that competitors used.
back.bio_text: Third-person, 3-4 sentences focusing on a notable career moment or accomplishment.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in a small box upper-corner.
back.distinctive_features: The 'rookie card' reproduction at the top of the back is the most unusual element. The colored field/grass-pattern background is unmistakable.

label: 1991 Pro Line Portraits
description: A 300-card studio-portrait set that broke from the action-shot conventions of football cards. Every card is a tightly composed studio headshot or three-quarter portrait against a neutral or stylized background, with a refined white lower nameplate and elegant typography. Looks more like a fashion-portrait series than a sports card set.

front.layout: Vertical. A large studio portrait fills the upper roughly 80 percent. Below the photo, a clean white nameplate panel contains player name, team, and position in a single horizontal layout. A small 'PRO LINE' wordmark in the upper-corner.
front.colors: Studio portraits with neutral gray or muted gradient backgrounds. Lower nameplate is clean white. Team logo in full color in a corner of the nameplate.
front.typography: Player name in elegant serif caps (a departure from the era's sans-serif standard). Team and position in smaller upright caps.
front.photo_treatment: Studio headshot or three-quarter portrait. No helmet, jersey only, often holding a football or with hands at sides. Neutral lighting, fashion-portrait quality.
front.borders: Thin clean white border on all sides. The nameplate is the main graphic element below the photo.
front.special_effects: None on base. Glossy matte finish.
front.distinctive_features: The studio-portrait approach with no helmets and a fashion-magazine quality of lighting/composition. Serif elegant typography is unusual for the era.

back.layout: Vertical. A second photo at the top (often a contrasting full-body or action shot). Below: bio text in elegant serif type and a small career stat line.
back.colors: White cardstock with serif black body type and color accent stripes.
back.stat_block_format: Career single-line stat summary, sometimes with a couple of recent years.
back.bio_text: Third-person, 5-7 sentences. More magazine-feature than almanac.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in the upper-corner in serif type, '47' format.
back.distinctive_features: Magazine-feature feel rather than data-card feel. Serif typography continues from the front.

label: 1992 Wild Card Football
description: The set that introduced the famous 'striped' redemption gimmick: roughly 1 in 100 cards came with colored numerical stripes (5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 1000) running along the borders, redeemable for that many copies of the same card. Visually defined by a glossy color action photo, an orange frame, and the dramatic colored-number stripes when present.

front.layout: Vertical. A glossy color action photo dominates the front, framed by a thin orange/yellow inner frame. Player name in a colored bottom bar. The 'striped' parallel cards have large bold numbers (5, 10, 20, 50, 100, or 1000) printed in colored stripes along the top and right borders, with the 'WILD' designation in a circular emblem.
front.colors: Black card face with an orange inner frame around the photo. Bottom bar in team or accent color. Striped versions add color stripes (each denomination has its own color: 5 is one color, 10 another, etc).
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif caps in the bottom bar. 'Wild Card' wordmark in italic. Large numerical denominations on stripes in bold sans-serif when present.
front.photo_treatment: Glossy color action photos, helmet on, mid-play.
front.borders: Black card-face background with thin orange/yellow inner frame around the photo. The numerical stripes are the unique extra visual layer.

label: 1993 SP Football
description: The set that introduced premium foil treatment to football. The first 18 cards (Premier Prospects rookie subset) feature an all-foil reflective front, while the rest of the set has a heavily-foiled metallic look with team-colored accents. The Drew Bledsoe rookie #9 from this set is one of the most iconic 90s football rookies.

front.layout: Vertical. The Premier Prospects subset (cards 1-18) has a full-foil reflective front with team-colored panels. The base cards have a foil-accented frame around the photo with team-colored top and bottom info bars. Rookie cards get a foil RC logo in the lower-right.
front.colors: Premier Prospects: full reflective silver/holographic foil background with team-color accent panels. Base cards: team-colored top and bottom bars with metallic silver/gold foil accents.
front.typography: Player name in foil-stamped caps. 'SP' wordmark in foil block caps. Team name in clean caps.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photos with a slightly cooler tone to complement the foil.
front.borders: Thin reflective foil border with team-color accents. Premier Prospects subset is foil to all four edges.
front.special_effects: Heavy reflective silver and gold foil. The Premier Prospects subset is essentially a foil refractor before refractors existed in football. RC logo foil-stamped.
front.distinctive_features: The full-foil Premier Prospects subset and the foil-accented frame on base cards. SP set the template for later foil-heavy releases.

back.layout: Vertical. Photo upper-left or top, bio in middle, stats at bottom.
back.colors: White cardstock with full-color printing and team-color accent bars.
back.typography: Sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stats, serif or sans for bio.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year college or pro stats with bold career totals.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number top-right inside a colored box.
back.distinctive_features: Premium full-color back with team-color accent bars.

label: 1994 Pinnacle Football
description: Pinnacle's flagship football release with a black-bordered design, gold-foil player nameplates, and a dramatic high-contrast photographic style. Known for its premium feel relative to the era and its distinctive jet-black borders that match the era's hockey card aesthetic.

front.layout: Vertical. Solid jet-black border around the entire card. Color photo inside, often with dramatic lighting. A gold-foil nameplate panel at the bottom holds the player name with the 'Pinnacle' wordmark in foil.
front.colors: Solid jet-black borders. Gold-foil accents. Color photo interior. The whole card reads as black-and-gold with photographic color.
front.typography: Player name in gold-foil sans-serif caps. 'Pinnacle' wordmark in foil block caps. Position in smaller foil caps.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photos shot with dramatic high-contrast lighting; many shots feel staged or artfully composed.
front.borders: Solid black border roughly 1/8 inch wide on all sides. Black background extends behind the lower nameplate.
front.special_effects: Gold-foil nameplate stamping. Glossy front.
front.distinctive_features: Black borders with gold-foil details. The whole card has a luxe black-tie quality.

back.layout: Vertical. Black background carries to the back. Player photo at the top, white text describing the player below, year-by-year stats at the bottom.
back.colors: Black cardstock back with white sans-serif text and gold-foil accent details.
back.typography: Bold sans-serif throughout, white on black for high contrast.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year career stats with bold career totals row.
back.bio_text: Third-person, 4-5 sentences focusing on a key career moment or stat.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in upper-corner in gold-foil type.
back.distinctive_features: Black-back design with white text - premium, magazine-feature feel.

label: 1995 Stadium Club Members Only
description: A premium parallel/insert of the 1995 Stadium Club base set, distributed exclusively to Stadium Club members. Same full-bleed photo design as base Stadium Club but with a distinctive 'Members Only' gold-foil stamp/seal, often with embossed elements and limited print runs.

front.layout: Vertical. Full-bleed color photo edge to edge. The signature Stadium Club ribbon at the bottom is enhanced with 'Members Only' gold-foil stamp and a wax-seal-style emblem.
front.colors: Full color photo. Gold foil 'Members Only' stamp in a corner.
front.typography: Player name in white sans-serif caps inside the ribbon. 'STADIUM CLUB' in gold foil. 'MEMBERS ONLY' in a separate foil stamp emblem.
front.photo_treatment: Premium Kodak-quality photography, same as base 1995 Stadium Club.
front.borders: No outer border. Full bleed.
front.special_effects: Gold-foil 'Members Only' stamp/seal. Premium glossy finish.
front.distinctive_features: The 'Members Only' gold-foil seal is the differentiator from base Stadium Club.

back.layout: Same as base 1995 Stadium Club back, with 'Members Only' designation added.
back.distinctive_features: Members Only stamp continues onto the back.

label: 1996 Pinnacle Mint
description: An iconic gimmick set: each card was paired with an actual coin (in bronze, silver, or gold) embedded or in a separate coin holder. The card itself featured a die-cut 'coin slot' shape and a metallic foil treatment that mimicked a mint-struck coin. Visually unmistakable.

front.layout: Vertical. The card design integrates a coin-shaped circular element either as a die-cut window or a metallic foil disc. Player photo fills the rest of the front, with player name and 'Pinnacle Mint' branding in foil.
front.colors: Heavy metallic foil - bronze, silver, or gold depending on tier. The coin element is a metallic disc with embossed or printed detail.
front.typography: Player name in foil-stamped caps. 'PINNACLE MINT' wordmark in foil. Coin denomination/tier label in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photos. Often the player is positioned to interact with the coin element compositionally.
front.borders: Metallic foil borders, often with deco or coin-edge ribbing details.
front.special_effects: Bronze, silver, or gold foil. Some versions had actual embedded coins. Die-cut shapes.
front.distinctive_features: The integrated coin disc element is the unmistakable signature. Mint-struck metal aesthetic.

back.layout: Vertical. Player info, stats, and a coin-themed graphic.

label: 1996 Topps Chrome Football
description: The first Topps Chrome football set, introducing chromium card stock and Refractor parallels to NFL collecting. The base design mirrors the regular 1996 Topps set but printed on a fully chromium reflective surface. Refractors add a rainbow-spectrum shimmer on top of the chrome.

front.layout: Vertical. Mirrors the 1996 Topps base set design - color photo with a colored bar at the bottom containing player name and team. Reproduced entirely on chromium card stock.
front.colors: Chrome silver underlying the entire card. Photo prints over the chrome with metallic highlights. Bottom info bar in team colors with chrome accents.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white caps. Team name and position in smaller caps. Topps Chrome logo in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photos printed on chrome - bright areas pick up reflective shimmer; dark areas read more standard. Helmet logos visible.
front.borders: Thin border (often white or chrome) following the base 1996 Topps design.
front.special_effects: Full chromium card stock. Refractor parallels add a rainbow-spectrum shimmer on the chrome surface that shifts under different angles.
front.distinctive_features: The full-chrome reflective surface. Refractor parallels add the signature rainbow-shimmer on top - this is the iconic Refractor look.

back.layout: Vertical or horizontal - matches base 1996 Topps. Stats, bio, card number.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stats, serif or sans for bio.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number top-corner in a colored box.
back.distinctive_features: Standard-style back; the chrome treatment is on the front only.

label: 1997 Donruss Studio Football
description: A studio-portrait set styled like Hollywood headshots. Players photographed in dramatic studio lighting, often without helmets, posed with theatrical flair. The signature Donruss Studio look: a sepia-toned or duotone treatment with elegant typography and a faux-frame border that mimics a portrait photograph in a frame.

front.layout: Vertical. A faux-photo-frame border (often beveled or with a matte) surrounds the player portrait. Inside, a studio-quality headshot or three-quarter portrait. Below the portrait, an engraved-style nameplate panel with player name and 'Studio' wordmark.
front.colors: Sepia or warm duotone wash on many cards, full color on others. Border in cream, beige, or a wood-grain pattern. Nameplate in elegant brass/gold.
front.typography: Player name in elegant serif caps engraved-style on the nameplate. 'DONRUSS STUDIO' wordmark in serif. Position and team in smaller serif caps.
front.photo_treatment: Studio-portrait photography with dramatic directional lighting. Helmets often off. Players posed contemplatively, hand-on-chin, looking off-camera, sometimes holding a football. Many cards have sepia or duotone treatment.
front.borders: Faux-photo-frame border, often beveled or with a matte texture. Cream or beige interior matte with the portrait centered inside.
front.special_effects: Foil-stamped nameplate accents. Some cards have actual texture on the frame.
front.distinctive_features: The sepia-toned Hollywood-headshot treatment. The faux-photo-frame border. Unmistakably 'Studio' branding.

back.layout: Vertical. A second photo at the top (often a candid or behind-the-scenes shot) and biographical prose below.
back.colors: Cream cardstock with serif black body text and brass-gold accents.
back.typography: Elegant serif throughout, like a magazine feature.
back.stat_block_format: Career single-line summary or short multi-row table.
back.bio_text: Third-person, 5-7 sentences. Magazine-feature quality, often discussing personality or style as much as stats.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in serif type in upper-corner.
back.distinctive_features: Magazine-portrait feel continues on back. The candid second photo is a Studio signature.

label: 1997 SPx Football
description: An ultra-premium die-cut, holographic, multi-layered set. Each card features a 3D hologram photo of the player set into a die-cut frame, often with a small secondary action shot. The combination of die-cuts, prismatic holograms, and foil treatment makes SPx instantly recognizable.

front.layout: Vertical with heavy die-cut detailing. A central rectangular or shield-shaped die-cut window holds a 3D holographic player image. Surrounding the hologram is foil-stamped frame work with the player name, team, and 'SPx' wordmark. A small secondary photo or graphic element often appears.
front.colors: Heavy holographic prismatic shimmer in the central image. Surrounding frame in foil silver/gold with team-color accents.
front.typography: Player name in foil-stamped caps. 'SPx' wordmark in distinctive italic foil. Position and team in smaller foil.
front.photo_treatment: The player image is a 3D hologram - when tilted, it shifts/animates. Surrounding photo elements are standard color action.
front.borders: Die-cut shaped borders, often with multiple cut-outs and foil-decorated edges.
front.special_effects: Die-cut, holographic, prismatic foil. Multi-layered construction.
front.distinctive_features: The 3D hologram in the central die-cut window is the unmistakable signature. Heavy foil frame work and die-cut shapes throughout.

back.layout: Vertical. Player info, stats, and a small holographic accent.
back.colors: Holographic and foil background, white or dark text.
back.typography: Bold sans-serif throughout with foil accents.
back.stat_block_format: Career stat summary, sometimes with a few recent years.

label: 1998 SkyBox E-X2001 Football
description: A multi-layered acetate-and-foil set that became the standard reference for translucent card design. Each card has three layers: a top layer with the player photo over a holographic silver shading, a middle clear-acetate layer with team-colored opaque curves and a transparent area for the logo, and an underlying base. The translucent acetate windows are unmistakable.

front.layout: Vertical. The card has visible layered construction. Top portion (roughly 1/3) has the player photo bleeding into a silver holographic shading area. The middle and lower portion is a clear acetate panel with a curved opaque colored sweep on one side (in team colors) and a fully transparent area where the 'E-X 2001' logo, player nameplate, team, and jersey number are gold-foil stamped.
front.colors: Silver holographic shading in the upper portion. Team-colored opaque curve through the middle. Clear acetate showing through. Gold foil stamping for type.
front.typography: 'E-X 2001' logo in distinctive futuristic gold-foil block lettering. Player name in gold-foil sans-serif caps. Jersey number in large gold-foil numerals.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo of the player, bleeding into the silver holographic shading at the top of the card.
front.borders: No traditional borders - the acetate edges are the borders. Some cards have die-cut detailing.
front.special_effects: Translucent acetate construction. Silver holographic foil shading. Gold foil stamping. Multi-layered visible depth.
front.distinctive_features: The clear-acetate window is the singular, unmistakable feature. The gold-foil futuristic 'E-X 2001' wordmark.

back.layout: Vertical. Same translucent acetate construction visible from the back. Player info, stats, and graphic elements gold-foiled or printed on the acetate.
back.colors: Translucent acetate with gold-foil and team-color elements.
back.stat_block_format: Career stat summary or short multi-row.

    – Single-game data → render as a small TIGHT STAT PANEL: a tabular block (column headers + one row of numbers) or a single inline ribbon ("33/37 · 431 YDS · 7 TD"). Not a vertical labelled list.

The silver holographic shading at the top of the photo zone with diagonal line patterns is also key. The gold foil futuristic typography distinguishes E-X2001 from other 90s premium sets.
iconic_examples: Peyton Manning rookie, Randy Moss rookie, Charles Woodson rookie, Brett Favre.

label: 1998 Bowman Chrome Football
description: Bowman Chrome's football debut, establishing the rookie-card-driven flagship that the brand became. Mirrors the regular 1998 Bowman set design but printed on full chromium card stock with Refractor parallels. The 1998 Peyton Manning Bowman Chrome rookie is one of the most valuable football cards of the modern era.

front.layout: Vertical. Mirrors regular 1998 Bowman: a color photo with the 'BOWMAN' wordmark in the top portion and a colored bottom info panel containing player name, team, and position. Reproduced on full chromium card stock.
front.colors: Chrome silver underlying everything. Photo printing has metallic highlights. Bottom info panel in team colors with chrome accents.
front.typography: 'BOWMAN' wordmark in bold serif block caps with foil treatment. Player name in bold sans-serif caps below. 'CHROME' or 'ROOKIE' callouts in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo printed on chrome - bright areas pick up reflective shimmer. Often pre-NFL draft photography in college uniforms for rookies.
front.borders: Thin chrome or white border.
front.special_effects: Full chromium card stock. Refractor parallels add the rainbow-spectrum shimmer.
front.distinctive_features: The 'BOWMAN' wordmark in serif block caps prominently across the top. The Refractor parallel is the iconic chase.

back.layout: Vertical. Standard back with bio, stats, and card number.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing.
back.typography: Block sans-serif headers, condensed caps for stats, sans for bio.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year college stats for rookies, pro stats for veterans.
back.distinctive_features: Standard-style back; chrome treatment is on the front.

label: 1999 Topps Finest Football
description: Topps Finest's late-90s peak. Mirror-finish chromium card stock with intricate die-cut frame designs and protective peelable plastic film coating. Refractor parallels add prismatic shimmer. The set is known for its complex graphic frame work and signature peelable protective coating that collectors often left on.

front.layout: Vertical. An ornate die-cut frame surrounds a central player photo. Multiple decorative graphic elements (lines, geometric patterns) layer around the photo. Player name in foil at the bottom or in a banner.
front.colors: Chrome silver base with team-color accents in the frame work. Foil typography.
front.typography: 'FINEST' wordmark in foil block caps. Player name in foil sans-serif. Decorative geometric type elements.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo, often with motion blur or dynamic composition.
front.borders: Ornate die-cut frame with multiple decorative shapes and openings. Sometimes with cut-out negative spaces.
front.special_effects: Chromium mirror finish, peelable protective plastic coating, optional Refractor rainbow shimmer.
front.distinctive_features: The complex die-cut frame work with multiple decorative geometric elements is the signature. The peelable plastic protective film is a unique era detail.

back.layout: Vertical. Stat-heavy with bio prose and card number.
back.colors: Foil-accented background with white or dark text.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year career stats with bold totals.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in foil, top corner.

label: 2000 Topps Chrome Football Refractor
description: The Tom Brady rookie year. The base 2000 Topps Chrome design follows the regular 2000 Topps look with a thin colored frame and team logo, reproduced on chromium with the Refractor parallel adding rainbow shimmer. The 2000 Topps Chrome Refractor Tom Brady rookie is one of the iconic modern football cards.

front.layout: Vertical. Mirrors regular 2000 Topps. Color photo dominant, with a thin team-colored frame inside the chromium edges. Team logo in a corner. Player name and position in a colored bottom bar.
front.colors: Chrome silver base. Team-color frame inside. Bottom bar in team primary color with white reverse-out type.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white caps. 'TOPPS CHROME' wordmark in foil. Team name in smaller caps.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo printed on chrome with reflective metallic highlights in bright areas.
front.borders: Chromium with thin team-colored interior frame.
front.special_effects: Full chromium card stock. Refractor parallels add rainbow-spectrum shimmer that shifts under angle.
front.distinctive_features: The Refractor's prismatic rainbow shimmer is the iconic visual signature. Tom Brady's RC has made this visual one of the most recognized in the modern hobby.

back.layout: Vertical. Standard back with bio, stats, card number.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number top-corner in a colored box.

label: 2001 Topps Heritage Football
description: A throwback set that recreates the design of 1957 Topps football - the second-ever NFL Topps set - with current players. Horizontal card with a player photo paired with a team logo or secondary photo, in mid-50s color blocks and serif typography. A nostalgic visual departure from anything else in the early 2000s hobby.

front.layout: Horizontal orientation (unusual for the era). Two-photo composition: a color player portrait on one side, a team helmet or secondary photo on the other. A solid color background panel between them. Player name and team in classic serif typography across the bottom.
front.colors: Mid-1950s palette: muted blues, greens, yellows, reds. Solid color backgrounds with cream or off-white outer borders. Team helmet in full color.
front.typography: Player name in serif block caps in cream-colored bottom panel. Team city in smaller serif caps. Position abbreviation in a circle.
front.photo_treatment: Color photo with a slight retro warmth. Helmets often visible. Some cards have a hand-tinted feel intentionally added.
front.borders: Cream or off-white outer border, classic 1950s thickness (about 1/8 inch). Color blocks within.
front.special_effects: None. Matte heritage finish.

label: 2003 Topps Pristine Football
description: A high-end set delivered in protective hard plastic encapsulation right from the pack. Each card came inside a sealed plastic case that doubled as a permanent slab. The design itself is a glossy chromium-style with team-color accents and elegant typography, designed to look pristine when slabbed.

front.layout: Vertical. Color photo dominant, surrounded by an elegant team-colored frame with foil accents. Player name in foil at the bottom. Pristine wordmark in foil at the top.
front.colors: Chromium silver base with team-colored frame and foil accents.
front.typography: 'PRISTINE' wordmark in foil block caps. Player name in foil sans-serif caps.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo with sharp clarity, pristine printing.
front.borders: Team-colored frame with foil accents.
front.special_effects: Sealed in a protective plastic case from the pack. Chromium foil accents. Refractor parallels exist.
front.distinctive_features: The pre-encapsulated plastic protective case is a Pristine signature. Premium chromium look inside.

back.layout: Vertical. Standard premium back with bio and stats.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color and team-colored accents.

label: 2004 SP Authentic Football
description: Upper Deck's flagship rookie-autograph product of the 2000s. Defined by elegant white-bordered cards with foil-accented frame work, on-card autographs for rookies (with a distinctive shadowed signature panel), and the 'SP' wordmark in italic block caps. The 2004 SP Authentic Eli Manning, Philip Rivers, and Ben Roethlisberger rookie autos are era-defining.

front.typography: Italic 'SP' wordmark in foil. Player name in foil sans-serif caps. 'AUTHENTIC' in foil block caps.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo with sharp clarity. Often premium-quality photography.
front.borders: White outer border with foil-accented inner frame.
front.special_effects: Foil accents, on-card autograph signature panels for rookies, serial-numbering for parallels.
front.distinctive_features: The white outer border with foil interior frame is the signature SP Authentic look. The shadowed signature panel for rookie autos.

back.layout: Vertical. Photo, bio, and stat info.
back.distinctive_features: Clean premium back with team-color accents.

label: 2005 Topps Total Football
description: The set that aimed to include EVERY player on every active roster (~600+ cards). Visually defined by a horizontal split design with a small player photo on one side, a colored team-info panel on the other, and a comprehensive stat-back. Looks more like a database card than a hobby card - and that's the appeal.

front.layout: Vertical. Color photo on the upper portion. A horizontal team-colored band separates the photo from the lower info panel that contains player name, team, and position in plain typography.
front.colors: Team-color band across the middle. Photo and info panel colors are clean and utilitarian.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif caps in the bottom panel. Team and position in smaller caps. 'TOPPS TOTAL' wordmark in a corner.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo, helmet on, mid-play. Often action shots from training camp or sideline.
front.borders: Thin clean border. Utilitarian feel.
front.special_effects: None on base. Some parallels exist.
front.distinctive_features: The utilitarian, almost-textbook look is the signature. Total roster coverage means lower-tier players who never appeared elsewhere.

back.layout: Vertical. STAT-HEAVY back. Comprehensive year-by-year stats are the main feature, often filling most of the back. Brief bio.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color and team-color headers.
back.typography: Condensed caps for stats throughout. Sans-serif body.
back.stat_block_format: Comprehensive year-by-year, sometimes including practice-squad info or split college/pro stats. The most stat-rich back of any modern set.
back.bio_text: Third-person, 1-2 sentences only - stats are the focus.
back.distinctive_features: Stat-table density is the unique feature - the back looks like a baseball-encyclopedia entry.

label: 2008 Topps Triple Threads Football
description: A super-premium memorabilia-driven set known for its die-cut letter/word cutouts that hold three relic swatches. The base design is heavy-foil with a jewel-encrusted frame work and serial numbering. Visually identifiable by the die-cut letters or words spelling out a relevant phrase ('STAR' / 'MVP' / 'CHAMP' / etc) embedded with relic patches.

front.layout: Vertical. Heavy ornate foil frame around a player photo. For the iconic Triple Threads memorabilia versions, three die-cut letter or word shapes (spelling 'STAR' or similar) are cut out of the lower portion, with relic patches visible behind each cutout.
front.colors: Heavy gold or silver foil base with team-colored accents. Black or dark backgrounds emphasize the foil.
front.typography: 'TRIPLE THREADS' wordmark in foil block caps. Player name in foil sans-serif caps. The die-cut letters are massive design elements, in foil-stamped block lettering.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo, often in the upper portion of the card, with the lower portion taken up by die-cut letters and relic windows.
front.borders: Ornate foil-stamped frame with decorative jeweled-style accents.
front.special_effects: Die-cut letter/word cutouts holding visible relic swatches. Heavy foil. Serial numbering. Sapphire/Ruby/Emerald colored parallels.
front.distinctive_features: The die-cut letter cutouts holding relic patches are the unmistakable signature - no other set looks like this.

back.layout: Vertical. Player info, stat summary, serial numbering. Often displays the relic provenance.
back.colors: Foil-accented dark background with white text.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number with serial number in foil, 'No. 47/99' format.
back.distinctive_features: Heavy foil dark back with serial numbering prominently displayed.

label: 2012 Panini Prizm Football
description: The debut Prizm set, establishing the brand that would become Panini's flagship modern football product. A textured chromium-style design with a thin team-colored frame, team logo at the top, and player nameplate at the bottom. The Silver Prizm parallel (just called 'Prizm' in 2012) adds the rainbow Refractor-style shimmer that defines the brand. Russell Wilson, Andrew Luck, RG3 rookies are the headline cards.

front.special_effects: Textured chromium base. Silver Prizm parallel adds rainbow refractor shimmer. Multiple colored Prizm parallels (Red Die-Cut, Green, Gold #/10, Finite 1/1).
front.distinctive_features: The textured chromium look is distinctive vs. Topps Chrome's smoother chromium. The 'PRIZM' wordmark and team-logo-at-top layout.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller player image, vital stats, year-by-year career stats, NFL/NFLPA logos at the bottom.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year career stats with bold career totals.
back.distinctive_features: Standard modern Panini back with NFL/NFLPA logos prominently.

label: 2014 Panini Prizm Cracked Ice
set_name: Panini Prizm Football, Cracked Ice Parallel
description: An iconic Prizm parallel adding a frozen-shattered-glass overlay across the entire card surface. The Cracked Ice pattern looks like fractured ice, with visible crack lines radiating across the chromium base, combined with the standard Prizm rainbow refractor shimmer underneath. Numbered to /25 in this year. The parallel that turned Prizm parallel-chasing into a hobby phenomenon.

front.layout: Same structural layout as base 2014 Prizm: textured chromium frame, team logo upper-left, player photo center, name and team across the bottom. The Cracked Ice overlay is applied across the entire card.
front.colors: Frozen prismatic refractor base in icy silver-blue-rainbow tones with the shattered-glass crack pattern overlaid in darker fracture lines. Team color accents bleed through the ice pattern.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white block caps at the bottom. PRIZM wordmark in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color on-field action photo with the Cracked Ice pattern overlaid across the entire surface including the photo itself. The photo remains visible through the ice texture.
front.borders: Textured chromium edge with cracked-ice fracturing throughout.
front.special_effects: The signature SHATTERED GLASS / FROZEN ICE pattern: visible crack lines fracturing across the surface like cracked ice on a frozen pond, combined with rainbow refractor shimmer underneath. Numbered to /25 on the back.
front.distinctive_features: The shattered glass/ice fracture pattern is unmistakable. Crack lines radiate across the entire card front including the photo.

back.layout: Same as base 2014 Prizm back. Smaller image, stat table, NFL/NFLPA logos. Hand-numbered or stamp-numbered '##/25' in red ink.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing. Red serial number stamp.
back.typography: Bold sans-serif. Serial number in distinct red.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number plus serial number '##/25' stamped or printed in red.
back.distinctive_features: The red /25 serial number identifies the Cracked Ice parallel.

label: 2015 Panini Select Football
set_name: Panini Select Football (Concourse / Premier / Club tiers)
description: A three-tier set built like stadium seating: Concourse (base, easy), Premier (middle, scarcer), Club (top, hardest). Each tier has the same chromium card structure but different framed photo treatments and color schemes that escalate in luxury. The card uses a die-cut window aesthetic with a frosted/etched edge effect around the photo, evoking the look of stadium architecture.

front.layout: Vertical. A chromium card with a centered photo enclosed in a die-cut-style framed window with a frosted/etched edge. Team logo and tier name (CONCOURSE / PREMIER / CLUB) appear at the top. Player name and position run across the bottom in a colored bar.
front.colors: Concourse: cool blue/silver chromium. Premier: warmer silver/red chromium. Club: gold/black premium chromium. Each tier has team-color accents.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif caps at the bottom. Tier name (CONCOURSE/PREMIER/CLUB) in stylized italic foil at the top. SELECT wordmark in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color on-field action photo, full bleed within the frosted-edge die-cut frame.
front.borders: Frosted/etched white frame around the photo, sitting inside a chromium outer card.
front.special_effects: Chromium base with refractor-style Prizm shimmer parallels (Silver, Blue, Red, Gold, Black, Tie-Dye). Concourse parallels are most common, Club parallels rarest.
front.distinctive_features: The frosted-edge die-cut window around the photo and the tier wordmark at top. The three-tier hierarchy is visually distinct: Concourse vs. Premier vs. Club have different chrome color schemes.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller player photo, year-by-year stat table, brief bio, Panini/NFL/NFLPA logos at footer.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing and team-color accents.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year career stats with career totals.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number printed in upper corner.

label: 2016 Panini National Treasures Football
description: Panini's ultra-premium high-end product. Thick stock, ornate gold-foil filigree borders, large rookie patch autographs, and cards numbered to extremely low print runs (often /99 or lower). The aesthetic is regal and museum-piece luxurious. Rookie Patch Autos with NFL Shield patches numbered 1/1 are the holy-grail chase cards.

front.layout: Vertical. Ornate gold-foil filigree border frames the entire card. Centered player photo (or a window with a jersey patch on RPA cards) takes the upper portion. Player name in elegant foil caps below the photo. Brand wordmark NATIONAL TREASURES in elaborate foil at the top or bottom.
front.colors: Gold-foil filigree borders on a deep base color (black, navy, or team-color). Photo in full color. Patch cards show colored jersey-patch swatches.
front.typography: NATIONAL TREASURES wordmark in elaborate ornate foil, almost calligraphic, at top or bottom. Player name in elegant gold-foil block caps. Position and team in smaller foil text.
front.photo_treatment: Color photo, often a posed studio or on-field shot. On Patch Auto cards, an oversize jersey patch window cuts into the design holding a real swatch.
front.borders: Heavy ornate gold-foil filigree border with vine-and-scroll pattern wrapping around the entire card edge. Premium thick cardstock.
front.special_effects: Heavy gold foil. Embedded jersey patches and on-card autographs on hit cards. Numbered to /99, /49, /25, /10, /5, /1. Various parallels (Holo Silver, Holo Gold, Black, Printing Plate 1/1).
front.distinctive_features: The ornate gold-foil filigree frame and the NATIONAL TREASURES wordmark are unmistakable. Cards feel and look like museum pieces.

back.layout: Vertical. Filigree gold border continues. Player image, brief bio, year-by-year stats, hand-numbered serial number prominently displayed.
back.colors: Cream or off-white cardstock with gold filigree accents and team-color highlights.
back.typography: Elegant serif or refined sans-serif. Hand-numbered serial in bold black or gold.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number with prominent hand-numbered serial '##/99' (or lower) printed or stamped clearly.
back.distinctive_features: Continued filigree frame on the back. Prominent serial numbering.

label: 2017 Donruss Optic Football
description: The chromium version of Donruss, sharing its design but on a glossy refractor-style chromium card. Bold team-colored frame with a curving arc design at the top. Pink Velocity, Blue Velocity, and Holo parallels are prominent chase cards. The Optic Holo parallel has a rainbow-refractor shimmer like Topps Chrome.

front.layout: Vertical. A bold team-colored arc curves across the top portion of the card. Player photo dominates the center. Player name and position appear in a bottom band. Donruss Optic logo appears at the top corner.
front.colors: Strong team color (e.g. saturated team primary) for the curved top arc. Black or chromium base elsewhere. Photo in full color.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif caps at the bottom. DONRUSS OPTIC wordmark in foil. Position abbreviation in a smaller call-out.
front.photo_treatment: Color on-field action photo, often with the player isolated from a darkened or stadium background.
front.borders: Thin chromium edge with a bold team-color arc at top. The arc is a defining shape element.
front.special_effects: Glossy chromium base. Holo parallel adds rainbow refractor shimmer. Pink Velocity and Blue Velocity parallels add color-tinted shimmer with directional streaks. Gold, Red, and other tiered parallels with serial numbering.
front.distinctive_features: The curving team-color arc at the top is the structural signature. The chromium glossy finish distinguishes Optic from base Donruss (which is non-chrome).

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller image of player, year-by-year stat table, bio paragraph, card number and Donruss Optic logo.
back.colors: White or off-white cardstock with team-color accents.
back.distinctive_features: Standard Panini back layout with Optic branding.

label: 2019 Panini Prizm Silver/Black/Gold Parallels
description: By 2019, Panini Prizm had a vast hierarchy of color-tier parallels. Silver Prizm is unnumbered base-rainbow refractor. Color tiers ascend in scarcity: Red, Blue, Hyper, Purple Power, Orange, Green, Camo, Light Blue Pulsar, Neon Green, Gold (/10), Black (/1). Each parallel keeps the same base 2019 Prizm design (textured chromium frame, team logo upper-left, player photo, name/team band at the bottom) but recolors the prismatic shimmer to the parallel's signature color.

front.layout: Vertical. Same as base 2019 Prizm: textured chromium frame, team logo upper-left, player photo center, player name and team band at the bottom. PRIZM wordmark in foil.
front.colors: Silver: full rainbow-spectrum refractor. Red: red-tinted prismatic shimmer. Blue: blue-tinted prismatic shimmer. Gold: gold-tinted prismatic shimmer. Black: black with subtle dark prismatic shimmer. Each tier shifts the entire Prizm rainbow toward its signature hue.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white block caps. PRIZM wordmark in foil. Position and team abbreviations.
front.photo_treatment: Color on-field action photo with the prismatic Prizm shimmer overlaid across the chromium surface.
front.borders: Textured chromium border with team-color interior accents.
front.special_effects: The defining Prizm rainbow-refractor shimmer, recolored per parallel tier. Gold parallel numbered to /10. Black parallel numbered 1/1. Mid-tier parallels have Pulsar (radiating streak pattern) or Power (vertical bands) variant patterns.
front.distinctive_features: Color-tier system is the defining feature. The Pulsar pattern (radiating streaks from a focal point) and Power pattern (vertical color bands) are visually distinct sub-types.

back.layout: Same as base 2019 Prizm back. Smaller image, year-by-year stats, brief bio, NFL/NFLPA logos. Serial number for numbered parallels.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing. Numbered parallels show printed or stamped serial.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in upper corner. Numbered parallels show serial '##/10' or '1/1' for top tiers.
back.distinctive_features: Serial numbering identifies the parallel tier on numbered cards.

Multiple color parallels (Silver, Blue, Pink Camo, Orange Fluorescent, Green, Red, Gold, Black) tint the mosaic effect.

label: 2021 Panini Contenders Optic Football
description: The chromium version of Contenders, featuring the brand's signature ticket-stub design on a chromium refractor base. Rookie Ticket Autos are the iconic chase cards. The design mimics a stadium ticket with a perforated edge, ticket header, and game-day information aesthetic.

front.layout: Vertical. The card is designed to look like a stadium ticket: a header band at the top says 'ROOKIE TICKET' or 'PLAYOFF TICKET' in bold caps, a perforated/dashed line runs along one side suggesting a ticket stub, the player photo dominates the center, and player name/team appear in a bottom band. Autograph sticker or on-card auto on hit versions.
front.colors: Chromium base. Ticket header in team color or contrast color. Photo in full color.
front.typography: Bold sans-serif throughout. ROOKIE TICKET / PLAYOFF TICKET / VETERAN TICKET in large block caps as the header. Player name in bold caps at bottom.
front.photo_treatment: Color on-field or studio action photo.
front.borders: Thin chromium edge. Perforated dashed line along one side mimicking a ticket stub edge.
front.special_effects: Optic chromium refractor base. Multiple parallels (Holo, Cracked Ice, Red, Blue, Gold, Green, etc.).
front.distinctive_features: The TICKET-STUB aesthetic is unique to Contenders: the bold ROOKIE TICKET header and perforated edge make it instantly recognizable. The autograph ticket cards are the chase.

back.layout: Vertical. Continues the ticket aesthetic with a stadium/gameday-information feel. Stats, bio, card number, and Contenders/Panini logos.
