label: 2008 Press Pass Power Pics Football
set_name: Press Pass Power Pics (College Draft Prospects)
description: A Press Pass insert set focused on dramatic full-bleed action photography of top college football draft prospects. Power Pics is one of Press Pass's photography-driven insert lines, emphasizing oversized dynamic action shots in college uniform with minimal design overlay. Press Pass held the exclusive license for college-uniform images during NCAA era when Panini and Topps could not show players in college gear.

front.layout: Vertical. Full-bleed dramatic action photo of the prospect in their college uniform. Minimal text overlay: a small foil-stamped player name and team band along the bottom edge, plus the POWER PICS insert logo.
front.colors: Photo dominates. Foil-stamped accents in school colors.
front.typography: Player name in bold foil-stamped caps. School name in smaller foil. POWER PICS logo in stylized foil insert branding.
front.photo_treatment: Dramatic full-bleed action shot in college uniform: helmet on, mid-play, often a tackle or rushing or throwing pose. Photographic emphasis on motion and intensity.
front.borders: No border, full bleed.
front.special_effects: Foil stamping for player name and insert logo. Some parallel versions add holofoil effects.
front.distinctive_features: Full-bleed action photo with school colors visible through the player's uniform. Subtle insert-set foil stamping rather than a bold design frame.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller player image at top, college stats, prospect bio, draft notes, card number.
back.colors: School-color accents on white or cream cardstock.
back.typography: Bold sans-serif headers, serif or sans-serif body text.
back.stat_block_format: College year-by-year stats with key categories for the position.
back.bio_text: Third-person, draft-prospect-focused, mentioning combine performance and pro projection.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number printed clearly with POWER PICS insert designation.
back.distinctive_features: Draft-prospect framing with college stats rather than pro stats.

label: 2011 Press Pass Football
description: Press Pass's flagship 2011 college draft prospect set, capturing players in their college uniforms before they entered the NFL. The base design uses a clean rectangular photo frame with school color accents and a bottom information band. Cam Newton, Andy Dalton, A.J. Green, and Julio Jones rookie cards from this set in college uniform are the iconic chase items.

front.layout: Vertical. Rectangular framed photo occupies the top three-quarters of the card. A colored information band runs across the bottom containing player name, position, and school. PRESS PASS logo and year designation in a corner.
front.colors: School-color accents (e.g. Auburn orange and navy for Cam Newton, Georgia red and black for A.J. Green). White or off-white border framing the photo. Bottom band in school primary color.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white caps on the school-color bottom band. Position abbreviation and school name in smaller text. PRESS PASS wordmark in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo in COLLEGE UNIFORM, helmet on, mid-play. School colors prominent. Photo crisp and clean.
front.borders: Thin white border framing the photo with a colored bottom band.
front.special_effects: Base cards are non-foil. Reflectors and Holofoil parallels add shimmer. Autograph and memorabilia inserts.
front.distinctive_features: School-uniform photography is the entire point. The school-color bottom band identifies the set quickly.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller player image, college stats, draft prospect bio, card number, Press Pass branding. NO NFL/NFLPA logos.
back.stat_block_format: College year-by-year stats with position-specific categories.
back.distinctive_features: College stats rather than pro. No NFL/NFLPA branding.

front_prompt_template:
PHOTO INSTRUCTION (this overrides any photo direction below):
I am attaching a screenshot of the player — that uploaded image IS the card's photo.
Use it AS-IS. Do not generate a new player, do not change the pose, expression, face, body, hair, helmet, eyewear, jersey, number, or uniform, and do not replace it with a stock or AI-rendered player.
Treat the uploaded image as a locked photographic asset placed inside the card.
Apply ONLY the card design described below — frame, borders, color treatment / tint / grain, typography, name plate, team panel, badges, logos, finish (matte / gloss / foil / chrome / refractor), halftone, print artifacts, etc.
Crop and position the uploaded photo to fit the card layout, but do not redraw the player.

YEAR INSTRUCTION (this overrides any year reference in the design block below):
The year that appears PRINTED on the card itself — copyright line, set year, season label, "© {{year}} <brand>", year inside any badge/banner, anywhere a year shows up on the printed card face — must be {{year}}. The design references below may name an older era (e.g. "1952 Bowman", "1972 Topps") to describe the visual style; that is the AESTHETIC source only. Do not stamp the era year on the card. Any year visible to a viewer of the finished card must read {{year}}.

CARD DESIGN:
A 2011 Press Pass football trading card front, standard 2.5 x 3.5 inches, vertical orientation. A rectangular framed photo occupies the top three-quarters of the card showing {{name}}, {{position}} for {{school}} in COLLEGE UNIFORM. School colors prominent. Thin white border around the photo. Across the bottom: a colored information band in {{teamColor}} containing player name '{{name}}' in bold sans-serif white block caps, position '{{position}}' and school '{{school}}' in smaller white caps. The 'PRESS PASS' wordmark in foil with year designation. NO NFL or pro team logos.

{{frontOverlay}}
back_prompt_template:
BACK CONTENT (data only — translate this into the visual language of the real-world set described in BACK DESIGN below):

{{contextStatBlock}}

YEAR INSTRUCTION (this overrides any year reference in the design block below):
Any year that appears PRINTED on the back of the card — copyright line, set year, season label, "© {{year}} <brand>", year stamp on a stat panel, year inside any badge/footer/banner — must be {{year}}. The design references below may name an older era (e.g. "1952 Bowman back", "1972 Topps back") to describe the visual style; that is the AESTHETIC source only. Do not stamp the era year anywhere on the printed card. Any year visible to a viewer of the finished card must read {{year}}.

BACK DESIGN — render the back the way the real-world set actually looked
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The text under "BACK DESIGN" further down describes the era, brand, color palette, layout, typography, and finish. Match it.

CRITICAL ANTI-PATTERNS — these are the AI mistakes that make the back look fake; do NOT do any of these:

✗ DO NOT add stock clipart / cartoon icons next to stat rows (calendar icon for week, helmet icon for game, whistle icon for result, football icon for passing, cleat icon for rushing, etc.). REAL production sports cards do not put cartoon icons in front of stat rows. Ever.

✗ DO NOT lay out the back as a vertical "LABEL : value" dashboard, e.g. "WEEK : Week 8 / GAME : vs UTEP / FINAL : 63-3 / RESULT : Win / PASSING : 33/37 / RUSHING : 4 car". That is a phone-app screen, not a trading card. On real cards, the matchup goes in a small game-info ribbon and the player's numbers go in a tight tabular STAT PANEL with column headers across the top.

✗ DO NOT render the BACK CONTENT block above verbatim. It is REFERENCE DATA. Translate it into the visual language of the era:
    – Multi-year career data → render as a multi-row STAT TABLE with column headers (Year, GP, and position-specific stat columns) and one row per year, the way the brand's actual cards did. Bold/accent the highlight row.
    – Single-game data → render as a small TIGHT STAT PANEL: a tabular block (column headers + one row of numbers) or a single inline ribbon ("33/37 · 431 YDS · 7 TD"). Not a vertical labelled list.
    – Bio → render as a flowing 1-3 sentence paragraph in the era's typical tone, not as labeled fields.

✗ DO NOT generate filler AI-recap prose ("carved up", "controlled the game throughout", "dominant performance", "took the field with conviction"). Either keep recap copy short and factual, or omit it.

✓ DO match the brand's actual era — typography, color palette, panel shapes, photo treatment (or absence), card-stock color, finish. A 2017 Donruss Optic back looks different from a 1972 Topps back; respect that difference.

✓ DO use the data in the BACK CONTENT block as the source of truth for every number, name, and date on the back. Do not invent stats or claims not listed there.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACK DESIGN (the original set's visual language — populate it with the data above):
Reverse of a 2011 Press Pass football card, vertical orientation. White cardstock with {{teamColor}} accents. Top: card number '#{{number}}', smaller The uploaded player photograph of {{name}}, position '{{position}}' for {{school}}. College year-by-year stat table: {{careerStatsTable}}. Draft prospect bio: {{bioText}}. Footer: PRESS PASS branding. NO NFL/NFLPA logos because this is a college-licensed product.
ai_generation_notes: Player MUST be in college uniform with school's helmet logo visible. NO NFL or pro team logos anywhere. School colors are the dominant color palette.
iconic_examples: Cam Newton in Auburn uniform, A.J. Green in Georgia uniform, Julio Jones in Alabama uniform, Andy Dalton in TCU uniform.

label: 2020 Panini Prizm Draft Picks Football
set_name: Panini Prizm Draft Picks Collegiate Football
description: Panini's collegiate-licensed Prizm product, featuring NFL draft prospects in their college uniforms. Same Prizm chromium aesthetic as the NFL flagship, but with college-team branding (school logos, school helmet logos visible) instead of NFL teams. Massive parallel rainbow (Silver, Red, Blue, Gold, Black, Pulsar, etc.).

front.layout: Vertical. Prizm chromium base with school logo upper-left, color photo of player in college uniform center, player name and school across the bottom. PRIZM DRAFT PICKS wordmark.
front.colors: Textured chromium base with school-color accents. Silver Prizm parallel adds rainbow refractor.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white block caps. PRIZM DRAFT PICKS wordmark in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo in COLLEGE UNIFORM with helmet on, school's helmet logo visible.
front.borders: Textured chromium border with school-color interior accents.
front.special_effects: Same parallel rainbow as NFL Prizm: Silver, Red, Blue, Hyper, Purple, Gold (/10), Black (1/1), etc. Refractor shimmer on parallels.
front.distinctive_features: College uniform photography combined with the Prizm chromium aesthetic. School helmet logo visible at the top corner.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller player image, college stats, draft prospect bio, card number, Panini branding. NCAA/college licensing logos at footer instead of NFL/NFLPA.
back.distinctive_features: College stats and college-licensing logos at footer.

label: 2022 Panini Phoenix Draft Picks College Football
set_name: Panini Phoenix Draft Picks Football (College Prospects)
description: Phoenix is a Panini collegiate-licensed product with a fiery, dramatic design featuring radiating flame/sunburst graphics emanating from behind the player. Bold colors, dramatic photography, and a rising-phoenix visual metaphor. Players are shown in college uniform with school colors prominent.

front.layout: Vertical. A radiating flame/sunburst graphic emanates from behind the player, suggesting a phoenix rising. Player photo dominates the center. School logo and player name and position appear in framing elements. PHOENIX wordmark prominent.
front.colors: Bold school colors with fiery red/orange/yellow flame accents. The radiating sunburst suggests fire and intensity. Photo in full color.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif caps. PHOENIX wordmark in stylized foil suggesting flames.
front.photo_treatment: Color action or studio photo in COLLEGE UNIFORM with the radiating flame/sunburst pattern behind the player.
front.borders: Thin frame with flame-graphic accents.
front.special_effects: Foil and chromium effects on parallels (Fire Burst, Yellow, Orange, Red, Blue, Purple, Green, Gold, Black parallels). Some parallels add intensified flame shimmer.
front.distinctive_features: The RADIATING FLAME / SUNBURST behind the player is the signature visual. The fiery, rising-phoenix metaphor distinguishes the brand.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller player image, college stats, draft prospect bio, card number, college licensing logos at footer.
back.colors: White or off-white cardstock with school-color accents.
back.distinctive_features: College stats and CLC/college licensing logos at footer.

label: 1990 Topps Football
description: A clean, white-bordered design with a thin team-colored frame around the photo and a curving banner at the bottom holding the team name. The TOPPS wordmark sits in a corner pennant. Emmitt Smith and Junior Seau rookie cards anchor the set. The design is more refined and less cluttered than late-1980s Topps.

front.layout: Vertical. White outer border. A thin team-colored frame surrounds the photo. Curving banner across the bottom holds the team name. Player name and position appear in a band beneath the photo. TOPPS pennant logo in upper-left corner.
front.colors: White outer border. Team-color thin inner frame. Curving team-color banner at the bottom. Photo in full color.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif caps. Position abbreviation in a smaller call-out. Team name in stylized caps on the curving banner. TOPPS in pennant logo.
front.photo_treatment: Color action or posed photo, helmet on for action, sometimes helmet off for posed.
front.borders: Thick white outer border with thin team-color inner frame.
front.special_effects: None on base. Glossy parallel exists.
front.distinctive_features: The CURVING BANNER at the bottom holding the team name is the signature shape element. The TOPPS pennant logo in the corner.

back.layout: Horizontal. Card number in a colored circle upper-left. Player name, position, vital stats. Year-by-year stat table. Brief bio paragraph.
back.typography: Sans-serif headers, condensed serif stats, sans-serif bio prose.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year stat table with bold career totals row.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in colored circle upper-left.
back.distinctive_features: Yellow/cream tone with the colored card-number circle.

label: 2022 Panini Score Football
description: Panini's affordable annual base set, the spiritual successor to the original 1989 Score. Bright team-color cardstock borders, dynamic on-field action photography, and a clean SCORE wordmark. Hot Rookies and Field Generals are signature insert sets. Multiple color parallels (Red, Blue, Green, Gold, Black) at varying scarcity.

front.layout: Vertical. Photo dominates the upper three-quarters with a thin team-color frame around it. Across the bottom, a bold solid color band holds the player name and team. SCORE wordmark in foil at one corner. Position abbreviation in a small badge.
front.colors: Saturated team primary color for the bottom band and frame. White or off-white outer border. Photo in full color.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white block caps on the colored bottom band. Team name in smaller white caps. SCORE wordmark in italic foil.
front.photo_treatment: Crisp on-field action photo, helmet on, mid-play, with stadium background slightly defocused.
front.borders: White outer edge with thin team-color inner frame.
front.special_effects: Foil SCORE wordmark. Color parallels (Red, Blue, Green, Gold #/50, Black 1/1) recolor the bottom band and accents.
front.distinctive_features: Solid team-color bottom band with player name in bold white caps. Compared to Donruss the design is cleaner and less geometric.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller image, year-by-year stat table, brief bio, card number, NFL/NFLPA logos at footer.
back.colors: White cardstock with team-color accents matching the front.
back.stat_block_format: Year-by-year career stats with bold career totals.

label: 2021 Panini Crown Royale Football
description: Visually one of the most distinctive sets in the hobby: cards are DIE-CUT in the silhouette of a royal crown. The card outline forms a crown shape with five points across the top. Regal purple and gold color schemes, ornate framing, kingdom-themed parallels (Imperial, Kaboom, Royal Court).

front.layout: Vertical, but DIE-CUT into the SILHOUETTE OF A ROYAL CROWN. The top edge has five pointed crown points. Photo within an ornate gold-foil frame. CROWN ROYALE wordmark in regal foil. Bottom band holds player name.
front.colors: Regal deep purple, royal blue, or team-color base with heavy gold-foil filigree framing.
front.typography: CROWN ROYALE wordmark in stylized regal foil suggesting royalty. Player name in bold gold-foil caps. Ornate decorative elements.
front.photo_treatment: Studio portrait or on-field photo, often with regal posed framing.
front.borders: Ornate gold-foil filigree frame inside the crown-shaped die-cut.
front.special_effects: Distinctive DIE-CUT CROWN SHAPE with five pointed crown points across the top. Heavy gold foil. Color parallels (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum), Kaboom inserts.
front.distinctive_features: The DIE-CUT CROWN SILHOUETTE is the defining feature: the card itself is shaped like a royal crown with pointed tops. Unique to Crown Royale and immediately recognizable.

back.layout: Same crown die-cut shape. Smaller image, year-by-year stats, brief bio, card number, NFL/NFLPA logos.
back.typography: Stylized regal foil headers, bold sans-serif body.
back.card_number_treatment: Card number in a small badge.
back.distinctive_features: The crown die-cut shape is preserved on the back.

label: 2020 Panini Playbook Football
description: A booklet-style product where cards open like a book to reveal multi-panel patches and autographs inside. The front and back are connected by a hinged spine. Iconic for its booklet format, Down and Out parallels, and X's and O's playbook-themed design elements.

front.layout: Vertical booklet format. Closed front shows photo with playbook diagram aesthetic (chalkboard X's and O's, route-running diagrams). When opened, reveals multi-panel inside with patches or autographs. PLAYBOOK wordmark prominent.
front.colors: Chalkboard-style green or black base with white chalk-line route diagrams. Team-color accents.
front.typography: PLAYBOOK wordmark in chalk-style font on a chalkboard background. Player name in bold caps. Route diagrams labeled in chalk-style text.
front.photo_treatment: On-field action photo overlaid on chalkboard playbook diagrams.
front.borders: Chalkboard-bordered frame with X's and O's pattern.
front.special_effects: BOOKLET FORMAT (folds open). Inside reveals multi-panel patches/autos. Down and Out parallels with serial numbering. Chalkboard X's-and-O's design language.
front.distinctive_features: The BOOKLET FORMAT is unique: the card is a folded booklet that opens like a small book. The chalkboard playbook design with X's and O's route diagrams is the visual signature.

back.layout: Closed back of the booklet. Smaller image, brief bio, card number. Inside panels (when opened) hold patches or autographs.
back.typography: Chalk-style font for headers, bold sans-serif for body.
back.distinctive_features: Booklet format with multi-panel reveal inside.

front.distinctive_features: The STAR-PATTERN corner accents and ROOKIES & STARS branding make this unmistakable. Cosmic theme on parallels.

label: 2020 Panini Revolution Football
description: A chromium parallel set with the iconic Astro and Cosmic parallels featuring kaleidoscopic prismatic patterns. The base design has a curving sweep with bold REVOLUTION wordmark. Parallel patterns (Astro, Cosmic, Galactic, Cubic, Lava, Sunburst, Fractal, Impact) create wildly different shimmer effects.

front.layout: Vertical. Chromium base with a bold curving REVOLUTION-themed graphic sweeping across the design. Photo within the curving frame. REVOLUTION wordmark prominent. Bottom band holds player name.
front.colors: Chromium base with team-color accents. Parallel patterns add wildly different shimmer effects.
front.typography: REVOLUTION wordmark in bold stylized foil. Player name in bold sans-serif caps.
front.photo_treatment: On-field action photo with player isolated.
front.borders: Curving sweep design with chromium edge.
front.special_effects: Multiple distinctive parallel PATTERNS rather than just colors: ASTRO (concentric kaleidoscopic circles), COSMIC (galaxy/star cluster pattern), GALACTIC (nebula swirl), CUBIC (geometric cube tessellation), LAVA (molten flow), SUNBURST (radiating rays), FRACTAL (recursive pattern), IMPACT (radial burst from focal point).
front.distinctive_features: Each Revolution parallel has a distinct PATTERN treatment, not just a color. ASTRO with concentric kaleidoscope circles is the most iconic.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller image, year-by-year stats, brief bio, card number, NFL/NFLPA logos.

label: 2020 Panini Tek Football
description: A reborn version of the iconic Stadium Club Tek aesthetic from the 1990s. Features tessellating geometric patterns (chevrons, diamonds, hexagons, lattice) overlaid in transparent layers across a chromium base. Pattern parallels create dramatically different visual effects: each card may have completely different background pattern.

front.distinctive_features: TESSELLATING GEOMETRIC PATTERN overlays are the entire identity of Tek. Each pattern variant is visually distinct.

back.layout: Vertical. Smaller image, year-by-year stats, brief bio, card number, NFL/NFLPA logos. Pattern continues subtly.
back.colors: White cardstock with team-color and pattern accents.
back.distinctive_features: Pattern designation noted on the back.

description: Real-time on-demand college football cards released within days of memorable college games or NIL events. Each card is print-to-order with a confirmed print run, then never reprinted. front.typography: NOW wordmark in bold red foil. Player name in bold sans-serif caps. Date stamp prominent.
front.photo_treatment: Specific moment from the game or event being commemorated, photographed in college uniform.

set_name: Topps Chrome Football, Pulsar Refractor Parallel
description: A Topps Chrome parallel adding a pulsing radial-burst pattern emanating from a focal point at the center of the card. The pulsar effect creates concentric rings of refractor shimmer that look like radiating light pulses from a star.

front.layout: Same as base Topps Chrome: smooth chromium base, team-color frame, photo, name across the bottom. Pulsar overlay adds CONCENTRIC RADIATING RINGS of shimmer from a focal point.
front.colors: Chromium base with concentric refractor shimmer rings radiating from a center focal point.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white block caps. TOPPS CHROME wordmark in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo with the pulsar radial pattern emanating from behind the player, suggesting they are the focal point of light pulses.
front.borders: Smooth chromium edge.
front.special_effects: PULSAR RADIAL-BURST: concentric rings of refractor shimmer pulsing outward from a focal point, like radiating star light or pulsing sonic waves.
front.distinctive_features: CONCENTRIC RADIATING RINGS from a focal point are the entire signature. Different from straight refractor or diagonal patterns.

back.layout: Same as base Topps Chrome back. Smaller image, year-by-year stats, brief bio, card number with Pulsar serial.

Any year visible to a viewer of the finished card must read {{year}}.

CARD DESIGN:
A 2024 Topps Chrome Pulsar Refractor parallel football trading card front, standard 2.5 x 3.5 inches, vertical orientation. Smooth chromium silver base. PULSAR RADIAL-BURST PATTERN: concentric rings of refractor shimmer pulsing OUTWARD from a focal point centered on the player, like radiating waves of light pulsing from a star. The uploaded player photograph of {{name}}, {{position}} for {{school}}. The player appears as the focal point of the radiating shimmer. Across the bottom: player name '{{name}}' in bold sans-serif white block caps, team '{{school}}' in smaller white caps, position '{{position}}'. TOPPS CHROME wordmark in foil.

CRITICAL ANTI-PATTERNS — these are the AI mistakes that make the back look fake; do NOT do any of these:

✗ DO NOT add stock clipart / cartoon icons next to stat rows (calendar icon for week, helmet icon for game, whistle icon for result, football icon for passing, cleat icon for rushing, etc.). REAL production sports cards do not put cartoon icons in front of stat rows. Ever.

✗ DO NOT lay out the back as a vertical "LABEL : value" dashboard, e.g. "WEEK : Week 8 / GAME : vs UTEP / FINAL : 63-3 / RESULT : Win / PASSING : 33/37 / RUSHING : 4 car". On real cards, the matchup goes in a small game-info ribbon and the player's numbers go in a tight tabular STAT PANEL with column headers across the top.

    – Multi-year career data → render as a multi-row STAT TABLE with column headers (Year, GP, and position-specific stat columns) and one row per year, the way the brand's actual cards did. Bold/accent the highlight row.
    – Single-game data → render as a small TIGHT STAT PANEL: a tabular block (column headers + one row of numbers) or a single inline ribbon ("33/37 · 431 YDS · 7 TD"). Not a vertical labelled list.
    – Bio → render as a flowing 1-3 sentence paragraph in the era's typical tone, not as labeled fields.

✗ DO NOT generate filler AI-recap prose ("carved up", "controlled the game throughout", "dominant performance", "took the field with conviction"). Either keep recap copy short and factual, or omit it.

✓ DO match the brand's actual era — typography, color palette, panel shapes, photo treatment (or absence), card-stock color, finish. A 2017 Donruss Optic back looks different from a 1972 Topps back; respect that difference.

✓ DO use the data in the BACK CONTENT block as the source of truth for every number, name, and date on the back. Do not invent stats or claims not listed there.

label: 2023 Panini Prizm Lazer Parallel

front.distinctive_features: The CRISS-CROSSING THIN GLOWING LINES forming a grid are unique. High-tech futuristic feel.

back.layout: Same as base Prizm back.
back.colors: White cardstock with full color printing.

label: 2024 Topps Chrome SuperFractor 1/1
description: The pinnacle of the Topps Chrome parallel hierarchy: a 1/1 SuperFractor with a wildly intense rainbow refractor pattern that combines all standard refractor effects at maximum saturation. The most valuable parallel in the standard Topps Chrome run.

front.layout: Same as base Topps Chrome design. The SuperFractor adds an INTENSE MAXIMUM-SATURATION RAINBOW REFRACTOR shimmer that is brighter and more saturated than any other parallel.
front.colors: Chromium base with extremely intense rainbow refractor: every color of the spectrum visible at peak saturation, shifting dramatically with viewing angle.
front.typography: Player name in bold sans-serif white block caps. SUPERFRACTOR designation. TOPPS CHROME wordmark in foil.
front.photo_treatment: Color action photo with the intense super-rainbow shimmer overlaying.
front.borders: Smooth chromium edge with maximum-intensity rainbow shimmer.
front.special_effects: MAXIMUM-INTENSITY RAINBOW REFRACTOR. Numbered 1/1.
front.distinctive_features: The INTENSITY of the refractor distinguishes SuperFractor from base Refractor or X-Fractor. Visually loud and saturated.

back.layout: Same as base Topps Chrome back. Card number with prominent '1/1' serial.

I am attaching a screenshot of the player — that uploaded image IS the card's photo.
Use it AS-IS. Do not generate a new player, do not change the pose, expression, face, body, hair, helmet, eyewear, jersey, number, or uniform, and do not replace it with a stock or AI-rendered player.
Treat the uploaded image as a locked photographic asset placed inside the card.
Apply ONLY the card design described below — frame, borders, color treatment / tint / grain, typography, name plate, team panel, badges, logos, finish (matte / gloss / foil / chrome / refractor), halftone, print artifacts, etc.
Crop and position the uploaded photo to fit the card layout, but do not redraw the player.

YEAR INSTRUCTION (this overrides any year reference in the design block below):
The year that appears PRINTED on the card itself — copyright line, set year, season label, "© {{year}} <brand>", year inside any badge/banner, anywhere a year shows up on the printed card face — must be {{year}}. The design references below may name an older era (e.g. "1952 Bowman", "1972 Topps") to describe the visual style; that is the AESTHETIC source only. Do not stamp the era year on the card. Any year visible to a viewer of the finished card must read {{year}}.

CARD DESIGN:
A {{year}} Topps Chrome Fourth and Inches insert football trading card front (fictional concept), standard 2.5 x 3.5 inches, vertical orientation. SMOOTH CHROMIUM SILVER base. CRITICAL: CHAIN-GANG YARD-MARKER (the bright yellow first-down chain-marker pole) prominently visible in the FOREGROUND of the photo. TENSE FOURTH-DOWN CONVERSION The uploaded player photograph of {{name}}, {{position}} for {{school}}, executing the fourth-down play, with the chain-gang marker pole in foreground emphasizing the do-or-die moment. '4TH & INCHES' GRAPHIC in bold {{teamColor}} foil overlaid in a corner. FOURTH AND INCHES wordmark in stylized intense foil at the top. Player name '{{name}}' in bold sans-serif white block caps, team '{{school}}', position '{{position}}', TOPPS CHROME wordmark in foil.

{{frontOverlay}}
back_prompt_template:
BACK CONTENT (data only — translate this into the visual language of the real-world set described in BACK DESIGN below):

{{contextStatBlock}}

YEAR INSTRUCTION (this overrides any year reference in the design block below):
Any year that appears PRINTED on the back of the card — copyright line, set year, season label, "© {{year}} <brand>", year stamp on a stat panel, year inside any badge/footer/banner — must be {{year}}. The design references below may name an older era (e.g. "1952 Bowman back", "1972 Topps back") to describe the visual style; that is the AESTHETIC source only. Do not stamp the era year anywhere on the printed card. Any year visible to a viewer of the finished card must read {{year}}.

BACK DESIGN — render the back the way the real-world set actually looked
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The text under "BACK DESIGN" further down describes the era, brand, color palette, layout, typography, and finish. Match it.

CRITICAL ANTI-PATTERNS — these are the AI mistakes that make the back look fake; do NOT do any of these:

✗ DO NOT add stock clipart / cartoon icons next to stat rows (calendar icon for week, helmet icon for game, whistle icon for result, football icon for passing, cleat icon for rushing, etc.). REAL production sports cards do not put cartoon icons in front of stat rows. Ever.

✗ DO NOT lay out the back as a vertical "LABEL : value" dashboard, e.g. "WEEK : Week 8 / GAME : vs UTEP / FINAL : 63-3 / RESULT : Win / PASSING : 33/37 / RUSHING : 4 car". That is a phone-app screen, not a trading card. On real cards, the matchup goes in a small game-info ribbon and the player's numbers go in a tight tabular STAT PANEL with column headers across the top.

✗ DO NOT render the BACK CONTENT block above verbatim. It is REFERENCE DATA. Translate it into the visual language of the era:
    – Multi-year career data → render as a multi-row STAT TABLE with column headers (Year, GP, and position-specific stat columns) and one row per year, the way the brand's actual cards did. Bold/accent the highlight row.
    – Single-game data → render as a small TIGHT STAT PANEL: a tabular block (column headers + one row of numbers) or a single inline ribbon ("33/37 · 431 YDS · 7 TD"). Not a vertical labelled list.
    – Bio → render as a flowing 1-3 sentence paragraph in the era's typical tone, not as labeled fields.

✗ DO NOT generate filler AI-recap prose ("carved up", "controlled the game throughout", "dominant performance", "took the field with conviction"). Either keep recap copy short and factual, or omit it.

✓ DO match the brand's actual era — typography, color palette, panel shapes, photo treatment (or absence), card-stock color, finish. A 2017 Donruss Optic back looks different from a 1972 Topps back; respect that difference.

✓ DO use the data in the BACK CONTENT block as the source of truth for every number, name, and date on the back. Do not invent stats or claims not listed there.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BACK DESIGN (the original set's visual language — populate it with the data above):
Reverse of a {{year}} Topps Chrome Fourth and Inches card, vertical orientation. White cardstock. Top: card number '#{{number}}' with '##/35' serial, smaller image, position '{{position}}' for {{school}}. Fourth-down conversion stats and game context: {{careerStatsTable}}. Description: {{bioText}}. Footer: TOPPS CHROME FOURTH AND INCHES logo, NCAA and team-conference logos.
ai_generation_notes: CHAIN-GANG YARD-MARKER POLE in foreground. '4TH & INCHES' graphic. Tense fourth-down conversion photography.
iconic_examples: Jalen Hurts QB sneak Fourth and Inches, Christian McCaffrey Fourth and Inches, Brock Purdy Fourth and Inches.

CRITICAL ANTI-PATTERNS — these are the AI mistakes that make the back look fake; do NOT do any of these:

✗ DO NOT add stock clipart / cartoon icons next to stat rows (calendar icon for week, helmet icon for game, whistle icon for result, football icon for passing, cleat icon for rushing, etc.). REAL production sports cards do not put cartoon icons in front of stat rows. Ever.

✗ DO NOT lay out the back as a vertical "LABEL : value" dashboard, e.g. "WEEK : Week 8 / GAME : vs UTEP / FINAL : 63-3 / RESULT : Win / PASSING : 33/37 / RUSHING : 4 car". That is a phone-app screen, not a trading card. On real cards, the matchup goes in a small game-info ribbon and the player's numbers go in a tight tabular STAT PANEL with column headers across the top.

✗ DO NOT render the BACK CONTENT block above verbatim. It is REFERENCE DATA. Translate it into the visual language of the era:
    – Multi-year career data → render as a multi-row STAT TABLE with column headers (Year, GP, and position-specific stat columns) and one row per year, the way the brand's actual cards did. Bold/accent the highlight row.
    – Single-game data → render as a small TIGHT STAT PANEL: a tabular block (column headers + one row of numbers) or a single inline ribbon ("33/37 · 431 YDS · 7 TD"). Not a vertical labelled list.
    – Bio → render as a flowing 1-3 sentence paragraph in the era's typical tone, not as labeled fields.

✗ DO NOT generate filler AI-recap prose ("carved up", "controlled the game throughout", "dominant performance", "took the field with conviction"). Either keep recap copy short and factual, or omit it.

✓ DO match the brand's actual era — typography, color palette, panel shapes, photo treatment (or absence), card-stock color, finish. A 2017 Donruss Optic back looks different from a 1972 Topps back; respect that difference.

✓ DO use the data in the BACK CONTENT block as the source of truth for every number, name, and date on the back. Do not invent stats or claims not listed there.
